perturbation.original_text: Representatives ” shall mean as to any Person, its directors, officers, employees, agents and advisors (including, without limitation, financial advisors, attorneys and accountants).
perturbation.changed_text: Representatives ” shall mean as to any Party, its directors, officers, employees, agents and advisors (including, without limitation, financial advisors, attorneys and accountants).
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Person' with 'Party' in the definition of 'Representatives' creates a contradiction. 'Person' is defined to include individuals and entities, while 'Party' is defined as Calpine and LS Power. This change makes it unclear if representatives of subsidiaries or affiliates are covered, as those entities are not 'Parties' to the agreement. It is contradictory when reading section 4.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Neither Calpine nor LS Power or any parent, subsidiary or affiliate thereof, shall be under any obligation to enter into any further agreements with the other signatory hereto or its parents, subsidiaries or affiliates of any nature whatsoever as a result of this Agreement. The Parties shall be free at all times to hold negotiations or enter into agreements with any other persons whatsoever (including with respect to projects under discussion by the Parties hereto) in addition to or in lieu of the discussions hereunder and any such activities shall not be a breach of this agreement or any obligations owed to the other Party hereunder. Each Party hereto reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to decline, to retract or to reject at any time any proposal which has not yet become legally binding by execution of a written agreement between the Parties with respect thereto, or with respect to any further agreements or business arrangements with the other Party hereto, its parents, subsidiaries or affiliates and to terminate all further discussions and negotiations.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Representatives' in relation to a 'Person,' which is broad and would likely encompass subsidiaries and affiliates. The changed text narrows the definition to a 'Party,' which is explicitly defined as Calpine and LS Power. This creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, which refers to the obligations of Calpine, LS Power, *or any parent, subsidiary or affiliate thereof*. Since 'Representatives' under the modified definition now only relates to Calpine and LS Power as Parties, representatives of subsidiaries or affiliates may no longer be covered, creating a direct conflict within the document and falling under the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' type as it is using the term 'Representatives' in a conflicting manner.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall cause all its Representatives to observe the terms of this Agreement and shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by it or its Representatives; and c. The Receiving Party shall return or destroy all Confidential Information (including all copies thereof) within thirty (30) days of receipt of a written request.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall cause all its Representatives to observe the terms of this Agreement and shall be responsible for any infraction of the terms of this Agreement by it or its Representatives; and c. The Receiving Party shall return or destroy all Confidential Information (including all copies thereof) within thirty (30) days of receipt of a written request.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'breach' is replaced with 'infraction'. While both imply a violation, 'breach' typically carries a stronger connotation in legal contexts, implying a significant violation of contract terms. Using 'infraction' suggests a less severe violation. This is contradictory because section 10 talks about breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Without prejudice to the rights and remedies otherwise available to either Party, each Party shall be entitled to equitable relief by way of injunction or otherwise if the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives breach or threaten to breach any of the provisions of this Agreement and the Receiving Party shall not plead in defense thereto that there would be an adequate remedy at law.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'breach' to 'infraction' in the Changed Text introduces a contradiction with the Contradicted Text. The Contradicted Text specifically uses the term 'breach' and outlines remedies available if a 'breach' occurs. By using 'infraction' instead of 'breach', the modified text creates ambiguity regarding whether the remedies outlined in the Contradicted Text would apply to an 'infraction'. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, as the interchangeable use of terms without clear definition leads to conflicting obligations and uncertainty about enforcement.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations imposed by Section 2 hereof shall not apply, or shall cease to apply, to any Confidential Information if or when, but only to the extent that, such Confidential Information: a. was known to the Receiving Party prior to the receipt of the Confidential Information; or b. was, or becomes through no breach of the Receiving Party's obligations hereunder, known to the public; or c. becomes known to the Receiving Party from sources other than the Disclosing Party under circumstances not involving any breach of any confidentiality obligation; or d. is independently developed by the Receiving Party, as evidenced by the written records thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations imposed by Section 2 hereof shall not apply, or shall cease to apply, to any Protected Data if or when, but only to the extent that, such Protected Data: a. was known to the Receiving Party prior to the receipt of the Protected Data; or b. was, or becomes through no fault of the Receiving Party's obligations hereunder, known to the public; or c. becomes known to the Receiving Party from sources other than the Disclosing Party under circumstances not involving any breach of any confidentiality obligation; or d. is independently developed by the Receiving Party, as evidenced by the written records thereof.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase "Confidential Information" is replaced with "Protected Data", and the phrase "no breach" is replaced with "no fault." This modification conflicts with the earlier use of "Confidential Information" in Section 2, where the Receiving Party is obligated to protect it. Furthermore, changing 'breach' to 'fault' weakens the obligation, suggesting a lesser standard of care is required. It directly contradicts with section 1 where 'confidential information' is mentioned, since the definition does not take place.
perturbation.location: 3. Exceptions to the Confidentiality and Non-Use Obligations .
perturbation.contradicted_text: " Confidential Information " shall mean all confidential or proprietary written, recorded, electronic or oral information or data (including without limitation research, developmental, engineering, manufacturing, technical, marketing, sales, financial, operating, performance, cost, business and process information or data) provided (whether such confidentiality or proprietary status is indicated orally or in writing, whether or not the specific words "confidential" or "proprietary" are used) to a Party (the “Receiving Party”) by the other Party (the “Disclosing Party”) in the course of the exchange of such information or data between the Parties. Without limiting the aforesaid, the existence of discussions between the Parties regarding the Proposed Transaction shall constitute Confidential Information hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Data' in a section outlining exceptions to confidentiality obligations. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which provides a specific definition for 'Confidential Information'. Since 'Protected Data' is undefined and used interchangeably with a defined term, it creates a misaligned terminology contradiction within the document, making it unclear which information is subject to the stated obligations. The shift from 'breach' to 'fault' further weakens the obligation and creates inconsistency within the document.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement is made subject to and shall be construed under the laws of the State of New York, without giving effect to its principles or rules regarding conflicts of laws, and that the state and federal courts situated in the State of New York shall have exclusive jurisdiction to resolve any disputes with respect to this Agreement or the Confidential Information with each Party irrevocably consenting to the jurisdiction thereof for any actions, suits or proceedings arising out of or relating to this Agreement or the Confidential Information, and each Party irrevocably waives its rights to jury trials with respect thereto.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement is made subject to and shall be construed under the laws of the State of Delaware, without giving effect to its principles or rules regarding conflicts of laws, and that the state and federal courts situated in the State of Delaware shall have exclusive jurisdiction to resolve any disputes with respect to this Agreement or the Confidential Information with each Party irrevocably consenting to the jurisdiction thereof for any actions, suits or proceedings arising out of or relating to this Agreement or the Confidential Information, and each Party irrevocably waives its rights to jury trials with respect thereto.
perturbation.explanation: The governing law is changed from 'New York' to 'Delaware'. This creates a direct contradiction because the agreement initially states it is under New York law. This change affects where disputes must be resolved and could impact the interpretation of the agreement. It creates confusion regarding where disputes need to be adjudicated between Parties in Section 9.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement is made subject to and shall be construed under the laws of the State of Delaware, without giving effect to its principles or rules regarding conflicts of laws, and that the state and federal courts situated in the State of Delaware shall have exclusive jurisdiction to resolve any disputes with respect to this Agreement or the Confidential Information with each Party irrevocably consenting to the jurisdiction thereof for any actions, suits or proceedings arising out of or relating to this Agreement or the Confidential Information, and each Party irrevocably waives its rights to jury trials with respect thereto.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that New York law governs the agreement, while the changed text states that Delaware law governs the agreement. The contradicted text restates that Delaware Law governs the agreement which means that the Changed text matches the contradicted text, and therefore the change causes a contradiction. The agreement cannot be governed by both New York and Delaware law, and this change creates a direct conflict and contradicts what was previously agreed. This falls under the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition of in-text contradiction.
